MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HUI DA CONSTRUCTION INC.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Cross-Complaint.

Defendant and Cross-complainant Hui Da Construction Inc.'s Motion for Leave to File Amended Cross-Complaint is GRANTED.

Defendant and Cross-complainant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 428.50(c). This section provides, "A party shall obtain leave of court to file any cross-complaint except one filed within the time specified in subdivision (a) or (b). Leave may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action." The inquiry, thus, is interest of justice." "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.) Courts are bound to apply a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint "at any stage of the proceedings, up to and including trial," absent prejudice to the adverse party. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.)

Plaintiff and Cross-defendant Chak Lung Siu has not demonstrated cognizable bad faith or prejudice. To the extent the amendment may expand issues or give rise to additional discovery, trial is more than six months away. In any event, the parties are free to seek a trial continuance on these grounds. Whether the amendments might give rise to a motion to dismiss or strike is not a question at this stage. Such matters are better presented by motion after the amended cross-complaint is filed. (See Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 760 ["the better course of action would have been to allow [plaintiff] to amend the complaint and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other proceedings"].)

Defendant and Cross-complainant must file the amended complaint within 10 days of entry of this order.

A proposed order is submitted for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
